mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.50% signal monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is in the lower tail of its historical distribution.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of 2016-02 indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.50% do not exceed mean returns when FEDFUNDS >= 0.50% by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth decelerates below 100,000 persons, signaling weakening labor market momentum that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice recession probability.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100,000 persons
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth < 100k are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal triggers in fewer than 15% of months but produces no directional edge.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline more than 10% month-over-month, indicating deteriorating residential investment demand that signals broader economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations are revised downward.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 546 thousand units as of 2016-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines > 10% are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal fails to trigger in any 12-month period despite economic weakness.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline simultaneously over a 3-month window, indicating synchronized supply-side and labor-market contraction that signals recession risk. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change < -0.5% AND PAYEMS 3-month change < 50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.21 as of 2016-02, PAYEMS at 130,311 thousand
regime_hint: synchronized supply-demand contraction or early recession
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both conditions are met do not show negative mean return (mean > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or the joint signal triggers fewer than 5 times per decade but produces no directional consistency.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% annualized rate while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 5.0%, indicating consumer demand weakness coupled with labor market deterioration. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate downward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 363,704 million dollars, UNRATE at 9.6% as of 2016-02
regime_hint: demand-side recession or post-shock recovery with persistent slack
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns when both conditions hold do not show negative mean return (mean > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines more than 0.3% month-over-month while federal funds rate remains above 0.25%, signaling deflation risk that forces monetary policy reversal. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as markets anticipate rate cuts.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 217.597 as of 2016-01, FEDFUNDS at 0.19% as of 2016-02
regime_hint: deflation risk with policy accommodation lag
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following deflation signals do not show positive mean return (mean < 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal triggers but subsequent rate cuts fail to materialize within 90 days.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month, signaling abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate equity risk-off and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as recession fears spike.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes > 0.3pp are not significantly negative (mean return > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal triggers fewer than 3 times per decade.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) remain below 600 thousand units, indicating weak aggregate demand and residential investment that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth < 1.5% AND HOUST < 600 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13,191.5 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-Q4, HOUST at 546 thousand units as of 2016-01
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns when both conditions hold do not show negative mean return (mean > 0%, t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
